reasoning: The statistical short-term trend from FRED suggests a relatively flat trajectory, placing the base probability of a short-term increase close to random walk (around 0.45-0.46 for April 20). Moving into the 1 to 3-month horizon (May 13 and July 12), the 3-month yield will begin to increasingly price in Federal Reserve rate cuts. Fixed income markets and CME FedWatch probabilities heavily incorporate 2 to 4 rate cuts through the end of 2025, starting likely in June or September. This macro expectation creates a downward drift on the 3-month yield, making an increase compared to April 13 less likely (giving probabilities around 0.40-0.42). However, as the forecast horizon expands to October (6 months), standard macroeconomic uncertainty increases substantially; factors such as reaccelerating inflation, fiscal policy changes, or Fed pauses make the long-term trend highly uncertain. Thus, in accordance with standard prediction scaling over long horizons with high drift uncertainty, the probability reverts closer to a purely uncertain 0.5 by the final resolution date.
